actionable_feedback: Provide a machine-readable export of the spreadsheet formula text for all key sheets (Inputs_Constraints, Requests, Allocation, Validation_Checks, Sensitivity, Final_Decision). Preferred formats: CSV or JSON containing columns: sheet, cell_reference, formula_text, displayed_value, and packet_row_citation.

Add a dedicated sheet named 'Formula_Text' (or equivalent CSV per sheet) that lists important derived cells and their exact formulas and the packet-row citations for rows used in allocation and validation. Include at least one explicit example per validation check (e.g., O_neg reservation, K-negative enforcement, irradiated allocation).

If openpyxl (or verifier environment) cannot parse the workbook, also export the key sheets as plain CSVs for values and a companion CSV containing formula text for the same coordinates so the verifier can deterministically validate logic.

Update the memo to cite the exported formula-text file and include a short excerpt showing the formula used to compute remaining stock and a formula used in a constraint check. Link the memo sentences claiming PASS/WARN/FAIL/NEEDS_REVIEW to specific cell references in the formula-text export.

Resolve grader/metadata score inconsistencies by choosing authoritative fields (recommend: final_score and passed boolean) and making failed_criteria / passed_criteria mutually exclusive and consistent. Ensure any automated grader metadata fields reflect the same pass/fail decision and numeric score.

Where the spreadsheet relies on assumptions or fallback policies not included in the packet (e.g., ABO/Rh compatibility fallbacks), mark affected allocations in Final_Decision as NEEDS_REVIEW and explicitly document the assumption in the memo.

After producing the formula-text exports and metadata fixes, re-run verification and attach the exported files to the attempt so the verifier can deterministically evaluate formula enforcement and shortfalls.

feedback_summary: Artifacts produced and memo content match rubric structure, but semantic verification failed because the XLSX could not be parsed in the verifier environment (openpyxl unavailable) and grader metadata contains conflicting score/pass fields. Key claims (formula enforcement of constraints, zero shortfall, row-level formula citations) are therefore unverified. Provide parseable formula/cell-text exports and fix grader/metadata inconsistencies before regrading.

grading_findings.score_consistency: inconsistent (final_score 0.6 vs metadata.score 1.0; failed_criteria and passed_criteria contradictory)

verifier_recommendations: Include CSV/JSON formula-text export for deterministic verification.
Ensure metadata fields are consistent and unambiguous before regrading.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce a revised attempt (615-revised) that includes (1) a formula-text export (CSV/JSON or a dedicated sheet) for all key sheets with cell references and formulas, (2) CSV exports of key sheets if .xlsx parsing is unreliable, (3) memo updates that cite formula-text exports and sample formulas, and (4) corrected grader/metadata fields so final_score and passed/failed criteria are consistent.
trace_specific_comments: Trace and stderr show attempted use of openpyxl and xlsxwriter and final fallback to OOXML packaging. Because the verifier environment lacks openpyxl, include plain-text exports as a robust alternative.
Agent trace records the openpyxl ModuleNotFoundError; use that step as justification for providing CSV/JSON formula exports in the revision.
